child_sections.title: There
child_sections.summary: There are no indirect effects of surtax on other taxes because it is the last tax in the import tax sequence.  Ministry of Finance, Ethiopia 15 Import tax expenditure report: FY 2018/19 – 2020/21 3.3

child_sections.title: During
child_sections.summary: During this period, the Ethiopian Customs Commission transitioned from the Automated System for Customs Data (ASYCUDA++) to the electronic Customs Management System (eCMS).

child_sections.title: Using
child_sections.summary: Using ECC standard rates, we obtain overall tax expenditures of ETB 40.0 billion (1.5% of GDP) in 2018/19, ETB 45.3 billion (1.3% of GDP) in 2019/20 and ETB 81.4 billion (1.9% of GDP) in 2020/21.

child_sections.title: Rate
child_sections.summary: Rate reductions are cases where a positive rate was applied below the standard rate.

child_sections.title: Indirect
child_sections.summary: Indirect effects of customs expenditure on the collection of other import taxes arises because customs duty payable is part of the tax base for all other import taxes.

child_sections.title: Relative

child_sections.title: Given
child_sections.summary: Given the longstanding objective of improving domestic resource mobilisation and the declining tax-to-GDP ratio over recent years, we should continue to monitor and evaluate tax expenditures.

child_sections.title: Whether
child_sections.summary: Whether costly tax expenditures help accomplish their objectives remains an open question.

child_sections.title: Two
